Once JS runs, `style.setProperty()` on `documentElement` takes cascade priority over `:root` stylesheet rules. --- ## Step 5 — `apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx` (NEW) ```tsx import { createContext, useContext, type ReactNode } from "react"; import { useTheme } from "../hooks/useTheme"; type ThemeContextValue = ReturnType; const ThemeContext = createContext(null); export function ThemeProvider({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) { const value = useTheme(); return {children}; } export function useThemeContext(): ThemeContextValue { const ctx = useContext(ThemeContext); if (!ctx) throw new Error("useThemeContext must be used within ThemeProvider"); return ctx; } ``` `useTheme()` continues to work anywhere without the provider (module-level singleton). `useThemeContext()` is an opt-in alternative for context-first code. --- ## Step 6 — `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` (NEW) Named export `ThemeSelector`. Takes no props; calls `useTheme()` directly. **Layout:** - `role="radiogroup"` wrapping `
` - One card per `ThemeId` from `BUILT_IN_THEMES` (3 theme cards) - One additional card for `"system"` option - Follows the existing `_chat.settings.tsx` card styling: - Selected: `border-primary/60 bg-primary/8 text-foreground` - Unselected: `border-border bg-background text-muted-foreground hover:bg-accent` - `role="radio"` `aria-checked={selected}` `

# Custom Theme System — Implementation Plan ## Context T3 Code's Appearance settings currently supports only `light | dark | system`. The goal is to introduce a proper named theme system with at least three built-in themes (`t3-dark`, `t3-light`, `t3-midnight`) covering seven semantic color areas, a React context provider, an updated `useTheme` hook, and a theme selector with color-swatch previews — all without touching build config or adding dependencies. --- ## Critical Files | File | Action | |---|---| | `packages/shared/src/theme.ts` | **CREATE** — types, constants, built-in theme data | | `packages/shared/package.json` | **MODIFY** — add `./theme` subpath export | | `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` | **REWRITE** — extend to ThemeId + CSS var application | | `apps/web/src/index.css` | **MODIFY** — add `--theme-*` CSS custom property defaults | | `apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx` | **CREATE** — React context wrapper | | `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` | **CREATE** — theme cards with color swatches | | `apps/web/src/routes/__root.tsx` | **MODIFY** — wrap `RootRouteView` with `ThemeProvider` | | `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` | **MODIFY** — replace radio-button Appearance section with `` | --- ## Step 1 — `packages/shared/src/theme.ts` (NEW) Pure TypeScript, no Effect dependency. Define: ```ts export const THEME_IDS = ["t3-dark", "t3-light", "t3-midnight"] as const; export type ThemeId = (typeof THEME_IDS)[number]; export interface ThemeColorTokens { background: string; foreground: string; accent: string; sidebar: string; editor: string; terminal: string; terminalForeground: string; statusBar: string; statusBarForeground: string; } export interface AppTheme { readonly id: ThemeId; readonly name: string; readonly isDark: boolean; readonly colors: ThemeColorTokens; /** Raw CSS custom-property values applied via style.setProperty() — no var() refs, no Tailwind functions */ readonly cssVars: Record; } ``` `BUILT_IN_THEMES` is declared `satisfies Record` (preserves literal keys, validates shape). `getBuiltInTheme(id: ThemeId): AppTheme` uses an explicit `if (!theme) throw` guard to satisfy `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`. **Never index `BUILT_IN_THEMES` directly** outside this helper. `resolveSystemTheme(prefersDark: boolean): ThemeId` returns `"t3-dark"` or `"t3-light"`. ### Built-in theme color values (all fully resolved — no var/alpha/color-mix) **t3-light** (`isDark: false`) - `--background: #ffffff`, `--foreground: #262626` - `--card: #ffffff`, `--card-foreground: #262626` - `--popover: #ffffff`, `--popover-foreground: #262626` - `--primary: oklch(0.488 0.217 264)`, `--primary-foreground: #ffffff` - `--secondary: rgba(0,0,0,0.04)`, `--secondary-foreground: #262626` - `--muted: rgba(0,0,0,0.04)`, `--muted-foreground: #737373` - `--accent: rgba(0,0,0,0.04)`, `--accent-foreground: #262626` - `--destructive: #ef4444`, `--destructive-foreground: #b91c1c` - `--border: rgba(0,0,0,0.08)`, `--input: rgba(0,0,0,0.10)`, `--ring: oklch(0.488 0.217 264)` - `--info: #3b82f6`, `--info-foreground: #1d4ed8` - `--success: #10b981`, `--success-foreground: #047857` - `--warning: #f59e0b`, `--warning-foreground: #b45309` - `--theme-sidebar-bg: #f5f5f5`, `--theme-editor-bg: #ffffff` - `--theme-terminal-bg: #f5f5f5`, `--theme-terminal-fg: #262626` - `--theme-status-bar-bg: oklch(0.488 0.217 264)`, `--theme-status-bar-fg: #ffffff` **t3-dark** (`isDark: true`) - `--background: oklch(0.095 0 0)`, `--foreground: oklch(0.985 0 0)` - `--card: oklch(0.105 0 0)`, `--card-foreground: oklch(0.985 0 0)` - `--popover: oklch(0.105 0 0)`, `--popover-foreground: oklch(0.985 0 0)` - `--primary: oklch(0.588 0.217 264)`, `--primary-foreground: #ffffff` - `--secondary: rgba(255,255,255,0.04)`, `--secondary-foreground: oklch(0.985 0 0)` - `--muted: rgba(255,255,255,0.04)`, `--muted-foreground: #a3a3a3` - `--accent: rgba(255,255,255,0.04)`, `--accent-foreground: oklch(0.985 0 0)` - `--destructive: oklch(0.65 0.25 25)`, `--destructive-foreground: oklch(0.704 0.191 22.216)` - `--border: rgba(255,255,255,0.06)`, `--input: rgba(255,255,255,0.08)`, `--ring: oklch(0.588 0.217 264)` - `--info: #3b82f6`, `--info-foreground: #60a5fa` - `--success: #10b981`, `--success-foreground: #34d399` - `--warning: #f59e0b`, `--warning-foreground: #fbbf24` - `--theme-sidebar-bg: oklch(0.12 0 0)`, `--theme-editor-bg: oklch(0.095 0 0)` - `--theme-terminal-bg: oklch(0.095 0 0)`, `--theme-terminal-fg: oklch(0.985 0 0)` - `--theme-status-bar-bg: oklch(0.588 0.217 264)`, `--theme-status-bar-fg: #ffffff` **t3-midnight** (`isDark: true`, GitHub-inspired navy) - `--background: #0d1117`, `--foreground: #e6edf3` - `--card: #161b22`, `--card-foreground: #e6edf3` - `--popover: #161b22`, `--popover-foreground: #e6edf3` - `--primary: #2f81f7`, `--primary-foreground: #ffffff` - `--secondary: rgba(255,255,255,0.05)`, `--secondary-foreground: #e6edf3` - `--muted: rgba(255,255,255,0.05)`, `--muted-foreground: #8b949e` - `--accent: rgba(47,129,247,0.08)`, `--accent-foreground: #e6edf3` - `--destructive: #f85149`, `--destructive-foreground: #ffa198` - `--border: rgba(48,54,61,0.8)`, `--input: rgba(48,54,61,0.9)`, `--ring: #2f81f7` - `--info: #58a6ff`, `--info-foreground: #a5d6ff` - `--success: #3fb950`, `--success-foreground: #56d364` - `--warning: #d29922`, `--warning-foreground: #e3b341` - `--theme-sidebar-bg: #161b22`, `--theme-editor-bg: #0d1117` - `--theme-terminal-bg: #0d1117`, `--theme-terminal-fg: #c9d1d9` - `--theme-status-bar-bg: #1c2128`, `--theme-status-bar-fg: #8b949e` --- ## Step 2 — `packages/shared/package.json` (MODIFY) Add to `"exports"`: ```json "./theme": { "types": "./src/theme.ts", "import": "./src/theme.ts" } ``` Follows the identical pattern used by `./model`, `./git`, `./shell`, `./Net`. --- ## Step 3 — `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` (REWRITE) Preserve module-level singleton approach (`useSyncExternalStore`) for flash-prevention and cross-tab sync. Extend the snapshot type: ```ts type ThemeSnapshot = { theme: ThemeId | "system"; // stored value resolvedThemeId: ThemeId; // system resolved to concrete id appTheme: AppTheme; // full theme object resolvedTheme: "light" | "dark"; // backward compat }; ``` **`getStored()`** — migrates legacy values: ```ts if (raw === "light") return "t3-light"; if (raw === "dark") return "t3-dark"; ``` **`applyTheme(stored, suppressTransitions?)`**: 1. Add `.no-transitions` to `documentElement` 2. Resolve `ThemeId` (call `resolveSystemTheme()` when `stored === "system"`) 3. Load theme via `getBuiltInTheme(id)` 4. Toggle `.dark` class: `documentElement.classList.toggle("dark", theme.isDark)` 5. `documentElement.style.setProperty("color-scheme", theme.isDark ? "dark" : "light")` 6. For each entry in `theme.cssVars`: `documentElement.style.setProperty(key, value)` 7. Remove `.no-transitions` in next `requestAnimationFrame` **`getSnapshot()`** — computes all derived fields; equality check compares `theme` AND `resolvedThemeId` (system preference can flip `resolvedThemeId` without changing `theme`). **`useEffect` dependency** — `[theme, resolvedThemeId]` so system pref changes re-apply CSS vars. **Return value** (fully backward compatible): ```ts return { theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme, resolvedThemeId, appTheme } as const; ``` `DiffWorkerPoolProvider` destructures only `resolvedTheme: "light" | "dark"` — unchanged. `_chat.settings.tsx` destructures `theme` / `setTheme` — type changes from `"light"|"dark"|"system"` to `ThemeId | "system"`, but that call site is being replaced anyway. --- ## Step 4 — `apps/web/src/index.css` (MODIFY) **In `:root` block** (after existing vars, before `@variant dark`): ```css --theme-sidebar-bg: var(--card); --theme-editor-bg: var(--background); --theme-terminal-bg: var(--background); --theme-terminal-fg: var(--foreground); --theme-status-bar-bg: var(--primary); --theme-status-bar-fg: var(--primary-foreground); ``` **In `@theme inline` block** (after existing `--color-*` mappings): ```css --color-theme-sidebar-bg: var(--theme-sidebar-bg); --color-theme-editor-bg: var(--theme-editor-bg); --color-theme-terminal-bg: var(--theme-terminal-bg); --color-theme-terminal-fg: var(--theme-terminal-fg); --color-theme-status-bar-bg: var(--theme-status-bar-bg); --color-theme-status-bar-fg: var(--theme-status-bar-fg); ``` These defaults use `var()` references (valid in CSS-level rules) and serve as the no-JS fallback. Once JS runs, `style.setProperty()` on `documentElement` takes cascade priority over `:root` stylesheet rules. --- ## Step 5 — `apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx` (NEW) ```tsx import { createContext, useContext, type ReactNode } from "react"; import { useTheme } from "../hooks/useTheme"; type ThemeContextValue = ReturnType; const ThemeContext = createContext(null); export function ThemeProvider({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) { const value = useTheme(); return {children}; } export function useThemeContext(): ThemeContextValue { const ctx = useContext(ThemeContext); if (!ctx) throw new Error("useThemeContext must be used within ThemeProvider"); return ctx; } ``` `useTheme()` continues to work anywhere without the provider (module-level singleton). `useThemeContext()` is an opt-in alternative for context-first code. --- ## Step 6 — `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` (NEW) Named export `ThemeSelector`. Takes no props; calls `useTheme()` directly. **Layout:** - `role="radiogroup"` wrapping `
` - One card per `ThemeId` from `BUILT_IN_THEMES` (3 theme cards) - One additional card for `"system"` option - Follows the existing `_chat.settings.tsx` card styling: - Selected: `border-primary/60 bg-primary/8 text-foreground` - Unselected: `border-border bg-background text-muted-foreground hover:bg-accent` - `role="radio"` `aria-checked={selected}` `

content.text:
Here's a summary of the full plan before you approve:

---

## What the plan covers

**8 files** — 3 created, 5 modified. No new dependencies, no build config changes.

### New files
1. **`packages/shared/src/theme.ts`** — Pure TypeScript. Defines `ThemeId`, `ThemeColorTokens`, `AppTheme`, the 3 built-in themes (`t3-light`, `t3-dark`, `t3-midnight`) with complete resolved CSS values for all 7 color areas, and a safe `getBuiltInTheme()` accessor.
2. **`apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx`** — Thin React context wrapper around the existing module-level `useTheme()` singleton. Provides `useThemeContext()` as an opt-in context accessor.
3. **`apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx`** — Theme cards matching the existing Settings page card style. Each card shows 5 colored swatches (background, sidebar, accent, terminal, statusBar) + a System card. Replaces the existing radio buttons in the Appearance section.

### Modified files
4. **`packages/shared/package.json`** — Adds `"./theme"` subpath export following the identical pattern of `./model`, `./git`, etc.
5. **`apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts`** — Rewritten to support `ThemeId | "system"`, apply all CSS custom properties via `style.setProperty()`, toggle `.dark` class, and migrate old stored values (`"light"` → `"t3-light"`, `"dark"` → `"t3-dark"`). Returns `resolvedTheme: "light" | "dark"` unchanged for full backward compatibility with `DiffWorkerPoolProvider`.
6. **`apps/web/src/index.css`** — Adds 6 new `--theme-*` CSS custom property defaults in `:root` and maps them into Tailwind's `@theme inline` block.
7. **`apps/web/src/routes/__root.tsx`** — Wraps the app content with `<ThemeProvider>`.
8. **`apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx`** — Replaces the existing 3-option radio group with `<ThemeSelector />`.

### Key design decisions
- The module-level singleton in `useTheme.ts` is **preserved** (flash prevention, cross-tab sync)
- `resolvedTheme: "light" | "dark"` return stays intact — zero changes needed in `DiffWorkerPoolProvider`
- All `cssVars` values are fully resolved (hex/oklch/rgba) — no Tailwind functions or `var()` refs, since they're applied via JS `style.setProperty()`
- `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` satisfied throughout via the `getBuiltInTheme()` helper with an explicit throw guard

2. **`apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx`** — Thin React context wrapper around the existing module-level `useTheme()` singleton. Provides `useThemeContext()` as an opt-in context accessor.
3. **`apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx`** — Theme cards matching the existing Settings page card style. Each card shows 5 colored swatches (background, sidebar, accent, terminal, statusBar) + a System card. Replaces the existing radio buttons in the Appearance section.

### Modified files
4. **`packages/shared/package.json`** — Adds `"./theme"` subpath export following the identical pattern of `./model`, `./git`, etc.
5. **`apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts`** — Rewritten to support `ThemeId | "system"`, apply all CSS custom properties via `style.setProperty()`, toggle `.dark` class, and migrate old stored values (`"light"` → `"t3-light"`, `"dark"` → `"t3-dark"`). Returns `resolvedTheme: "light" | "dark"` unchanged for full backward compatibility with `DiffWorkerPoolProvider`.
6. **`apps/web/src/index.css`** — Adds 6 new `--theme-*` CSS custom property defaults in `:root` and maps them into Tailwind's `@theme inline` block.
7. **`apps/web/src/routes/__root.tsx`** — Wraps the app content with `<ThemeProvider>`.
8. **`apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx`** — Replaces the existing 3-option radio group with `<ThemeSelector />`.

### Key design decisions
- The module-level singleton in `useTheme.ts` is **preserved** (flash prevention, cross-tab sync)
- `resolvedTheme: "light" | "dark"` return stays intact — zero changes needed in `DiffWorkerPoolProvider`
- All `cssVars` values are fully resolved (hex/oklch/rgba) — no Tailwind functions or `var()` refs, since they're applied via JS `style.setProperty()`
- `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` satisfied throughout via the `getBuiltInTheme()` helper with an explicit throw guard

tool_input.comment: Here's the complete implementation plan for the theme system. Please review before I proceed.
tool_input.plan:
# Custom Theme System — Implementation Plan ## Context T3 Code's Appearance settings currently supports only `light | dark | system`. The goal is to introduce a proper named theme system with at least three built-in themes (`t3-dark`, `t3-light`, `t3-midnight`) covering seven semantic color areas, a React context provider, an updated `useTheme` hook, and a theme selector with color-swatch previews — all without touching build config or adding dependencies. --- ## Critical Files | File | Action | |---|---| | `packages/shared/src/theme.ts` | **CREATE** — types, constants, built-in theme data | | `packages/shared/package.json` | **MODIFY** — add `./theme` subpath export | | `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` | **REWRITE** — extend to ThemeId + CSS var application | | `apps/web/src/index.css` | **MODIFY** — add `--theme-*` CSS custom property defaults | | `apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx` | **CREATE** — React context wrapper | | `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` | **CREATE** — theme cards with color swatches | | `apps/web/src/routes/__root.tsx` | **MODIFY** — wrap `RootRouteView` with `ThemeProvider` | | `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx` | **MODIFY** — replace radio-button Appearance section with `` | --- ## Step 1 — `packages/shared/src/theme.ts` (NEW) Pure TypeScript, no Effect dependency. Define: ```ts export const THEME_IDS = ["t3-dark", "t3-light", "t3-midnight"] as const; export type ThemeId = (typeof THEME_IDS)[number]; export interface ThemeColorTokens { background: string; foreground: string; accent: string; sidebar: string; editor: string; terminal: string; terminalForeground: string; statusBar: string; statusBarForeground: string; } export interface AppTheme { readonly id: ThemeId; readonly name: string; readonly isDark: boolean; readonly colors: ThemeColorTokens; /** Raw CSS custom-property values applied via style.setProperty() — no var() refs, no Tailwind functions */ readonly cssVars: Record; } ``` `BUILT_IN_THEMES` is declared `satisfies Record` (preserves literal keys, validates shape). `getBuiltInTheme(id: ThemeId): AppTheme` uses an explicit `if (!theme) throw` guard to satisfy `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`. **Never index `BUILT_IN_THEMES` directly** outside this helper. `resolveSystemTheme(prefersDark: boolean): ThemeId` returns `"t3-dark"` or `"t3-light"`. ### Built-in theme color values (all fully resolved — no var/alpha/color-mix) **t3-light** (`isDark: false`) - `--background: #ffffff`, `--foreground: #262626` - `--card: #ffffff`, `--card-foreground: #262626` - `--popover: #ffffff`, `--popover-foreground: #262626` - `--primary: oklch(0.488 0.217 264)`, `--primary-foreground: #ffffff` - `--secondary: rgba(0,0,0,0.04)`, `--secondary-foreground: #262626` - `--muted: rgba(0,0,0,0.04)`, `--muted-foreground: #737373` - `--accent: rgba(0,0,0,0.04)`, `--accent-foreground: #262626` - `--destructive: #ef4444`, `--destructive-foreground: #b91c1c` - `--border: rgba(0,0,0,0.08)`, `--input: rgba(0,0,0,0.10)`, `--ring: oklch(0.488 0.217 264)` - `--info: #3b82f6`, `--info-foreground: #1d4ed8` - `--success: #10b981`, `--success-foreground: #047857` - `--warning: #f59e0b`, `--warning-foreground: #b45309` - `--theme-sidebar-bg: #f5f5f5`, `--theme-editor-bg: #ffffff` - `--theme-terminal-bg: #f5f5f5`, `--theme-terminal-fg: #262626` - `--theme-status-bar-bg: oklch(0.488 0.217 264)`, `--theme-status-bar-fg: #ffffff` **t3-dark** (`isDark: true`) - `--background: oklch(0.095 0 0)`, `--foreground: oklch(0.985 0 0)` - `--card: oklch(0.105 0 0)`, `--card-foreground: oklch(0.985 0 0)` - `--popover: oklch(0.105 0 0)`, `--popover-foreground: oklch(0.985 0 0)` - `--primary: oklch(0.588 0.217 264)`, `--primary-foreground: #ffffff` - `--secondary: rgba(255,255,255,0.04)`, `--secondary-foreground: oklch(0.985 0 0)` - `--muted: rgba(255,255,255,0.04)`, `--muted-foreground: #a3a3a3` - `--accent: rgba(255,255,255,0.04)`, `--accent-foreground: oklch(0.985 0 0)` - `--destructive: oklch(0.65 0.25 25)`, `--destructive-foreground: oklch(0.704 0.191 22.216)` - `--border: rgba(255,255,255,0.06)`, `--input: rgba(255,255,255,0.08)`, `--ring: oklch(0.588 0.217 264)` - `--info: #3b82f6`, `--info-foreground: #60a5fa` - `--success: #10b981`, `--success-foreground: #34d399` - `--warning: #f59e0b`, `--warning-foreground: #fbbf24` - `--theme-sidebar-bg: oklch(0.12 0 0)`, `--theme-editor-bg: oklch(0.095 0 0)` - `--theme-terminal-bg: oklch(0.095 0 0)`, `--theme-terminal-fg: oklch(0.985 0 0)` - `--theme-status-bar-bg: oklch(0.588 0.217 264)`, `--theme-status-bar-fg: #ffffff` **t3-midnight** (`isDark: true`, GitHub-inspired navy) - `--background: #0d1117`, `--foreground: #e6edf3` - `--card: #161b22`, `--card-foreground: #e6edf3` - `--popover: #161b22`, `--popover-foreground: #e6edf3` - `--primary: #2f81f7`, `--primary-foreground: #ffffff` - `--secondary: rgba(255,255,255,0.05)`, `--secondary-foreground: #e6edf3` - `--muted: rgba(255,255,255,0.05)`, `--muted-foreground: #8b949e` - `--accent: rgba(47,129,247,0.08)`, `--accent-foreground: #e6edf3` - `--destructive: #f85149`, `--destructive-foreground: #ffa198` - `--border: rgba(48,54,61,0.8)`, `--input: rgba(48,54,61,0.9)`, `--ring: #2f81f7` - `--info: #58a6ff`, `--info-foreground: #a5d6ff` - `--success: #3fb950`, `--success-foreground: #56d364` - `--warning: #d29922`, `--warning-foreground: #e3b341` - `--theme-sidebar-bg: #161b22`, `--theme-editor-bg: #0d1117` - `--theme-terminal-bg: #0d1117`, `--theme-terminal-fg: #c9d1d9` - `--theme-status-bar-bg: #1c2128`, `--theme-status-bar-fg: #8b949e` --- ## Step 2 — `packages/shared/package.json` (MODIFY) Add to `"exports"`: ```json "./theme": { "types": "./src/theme.ts", "import": "./src/theme.ts" } ``` Follows the identical pattern used by `./model`, `./git`, `./shell`, `./Net`. --- ## Step 3 — `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` (REWRITE) Preserve module-level singleton approach (`useSyncExternalStore`) for flash-prevention and cross-tab sync. Extend the snapshot type: ```ts type ThemeSnapshot = { theme: ThemeId | "system"; // stored value resolvedThemeId: ThemeId; // system resolved to concrete id appTheme: AppTheme; // full theme object resolvedTheme: "light" | "dark"; // backward compat }; ``` **`getStored()`** — migrates legacy values: ```ts if (raw === "light") return "t3-light"; if (raw === "dark") return "t3-dark"; ``` **`applyTheme(stored, suppressTransitions?)`**: 1. Add `.no-transitions` to `documentElement` 2. Resolve `ThemeId` (call `resolveSystemTheme()` when `stored === "system"`) 3. Load theme via `getBuiltInTheme(id)` 4. Toggle `.dark` class: `documentElement.classList.toggle("dark", theme.isDark)` 5. `documentElement.style.setProperty("color-scheme", theme.isDark ? "dark" : "light")` 6. For each entry in `theme.cssVars`: `documentElement.style.setProperty(key, value)` 7. Remove `.no-transitions` in next `requestAnimationFrame` **`getSnapshot()`** — computes all derived fields; equality check compares `theme` AND `resolvedThemeId` (system preference can flip `resolvedThemeId` without changing `theme`). **`useEffect` dependency** — `[theme, resolvedThemeId]` so system pref changes re-apply CSS vars. **Return value** (fully backward compatible): ```ts return { theme, setTheme, resolvedTheme, resolvedThemeId, appTheme } as const; ``` `DiffWorkerPoolProvider` destructures only `resolvedTheme: "light" | "dark"` — unchanged. `_chat.settings.tsx` destructures `theme` / `setTheme` — type changes from `"light"|"dark"|"system"` to `ThemeId | "system"`, but that call site is being replaced anyway. --- ## Step 4 — `apps/web/src/index.css` (MODIFY) **In `:root` block** (after existing vars, before `@variant dark`): ```css --theme-sidebar-bg: var(--card); --theme-editor-bg: var(--background); --theme-terminal-bg: var(--background); --theme-terminal-fg: var(--foreground); --theme-status-bar-bg: var(--primary); --theme-status-bar-fg: var(--primary-foreground); ``` **In `@theme inline` block** (after existing `--color-*` mappings): ```css --color-theme-sidebar-bg: var(--theme-sidebar-bg); --color-theme-editor-bg: var(--theme-editor-bg); --color-theme-terminal-bg: var(--theme-terminal-bg); --color-theme-terminal-fg: var(--theme-terminal-fg); --color-theme-status-bar-bg: var(--theme-status-bar-bg); --color-theme-status-bar-fg: var(--theme-status-bar-fg); ``` These defaults use `var()` references (valid in CSS-level rules) and serve as the no-JS fallback. Once JS runs, `style.setProperty()` on `documentElement` takes cascade priority over `:root` stylesheet rules. --- ## Step 5 — `apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx` (NEW) ```tsx import { createContext, useContext, type ReactNode } from "react"; import { useTheme } from "../hooks/useTheme"; type ThemeContextValue = ReturnType; const ThemeContext = createContext(null); export function ThemeProvider({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) { const value = useTheme(); return {children}; } export function useThemeContext(): ThemeContextValue { const ctx = useContext(ThemeContext); if (!ctx) throw new Error("useThemeContext must be used within ThemeProvider"); return ctx; } ``` `useTheme()` continues to work anywhere without the provider (module-level singleton). `useThemeContext()` is an opt-in alternative for context-first code. --- ## Step 6 — `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` (NEW) Named export `ThemeSelector`. Takes no props; calls `useTheme()` directly. **Layout:** - `role="radiogroup"` wrapping `
` - One card per `ThemeId` from `BUILT_IN_THEMES` (3 theme cards) - One additional card for `"system"` option - Follows the existing `_chat.settings.tsx` card styling: - Selected: `border-primary/60 bg-primary/8 text-foreground` - Unselected: `border-border bg-background text-muted-foreground hover:bg-accent` - `role="radio"` `aria-checked={selected}` `

content.text:
All 8 files are implemented and verified. Here's a summary of everything that was done:

---

## What was implemented

### New files (3)

**`packages/shared/src/theme.ts`**
Defines `ThemeId`, `ThemeColorTokens`, `AppTheme` types, and the 3 fully-specified built-in themes with all CSS variables pre-resolved to raw `hex`/`oklch`/`rgba` values (no Tailwind functions — safe for `style.setProperty()`). Exports `getBuiltInTheme(id)` with an explicit throw guard to satisfy `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`, and `resolveSystemTheme(prefersDark)` for OS-preference resolution.

**`apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx`**
A React context wrapper that calls `useTheme()` once and provides its result via context. Also exports `useThemeContext()` as an opt-in accessor for context-first code.

**`apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx`**
Settings UI component. Renders 3 named theme cards (T3 Light, T3 Dark, T3 Midnight) each showing 5 color swatches (background, sidebar, accent, terminal, statusBar) via inline styles, plus a System card. Follows the exact radio-button card style already in the settings page.

### Modified files (5)

**`packages/shared/package.json`** — added `"./theme"` subpath export.

**`apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts`** — full rewrite. Now supports `ThemeId | "system"`, migrates old stored values (`"light"` → `"t3-light"`, `"dark"` → `"t3-dark"`), applies all CSS custom properties via `style.setProperty()` per-theme on every change, and returns the new `resolvedThemeId`/`appTheme` fields while keeping `resolvedTheme: "light" | "dark"` unchanged for `DiffWorkerPoolProvider` backward compatibility.

**`apps/web/src/index.css`** — added 6 `--theme-*` CSS custom property defaults to `:root` and their Tailwind `@theme inline` mappings.

**`apps/web/src/routes/__root.tsx`** — wrapped the app with `<ThemeProvider>`.

**`apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx`** — removed `THEME_OPTIONS` const, removed `useTheme` import, replaced the 3-option radio group with `<ThemeSelector />`.

**`packages/shared/src/theme.ts`**
